conditions.title: Back Pain
conditions.description: From sudden onset to long-standing discomfort, acupuncture and TCM therapies offer meaningful relief for lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica—without relying on drugs or surgery.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, back pain is understood as an obstruction of Qi and blood within the channels. Our approach combines acupuncture to clear those obstructions, cupping therapy to encourage better circulation, and herbal medicine to ease inflammation and support back strength.

conditions.title: Neck & Shoulder Pain
conditions.description: Whether tension, stiffness, or pain stems from stress, poor posture, or an injury, we can help bring relief to the neck and shoulder area.
conditions.tcmApproach: Neck and shoulder pain is frequently linked to Qi stagnation and blood stasis in TCM. We address this through acupuncture to release trigger points, Tui Na to ease tight muscles, and cupping to promote healthy circulation.

conditions.title: Arthritis & Joint Pain
symptoms: Pain that intensifies with changes in weather
conditions.description: Natural pain management approaches for osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and generalized joint inflammation.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM commonly classifies arthritis as a 'Bi Syndrome,' where pathogenic factors such as wind, cold, dampness, or heat obstruct the joints. Treatment is aimed at clearing these factors, easing inflammation, and supporting the body's overall strength.

conditions.title: Headaches & Migraines
conditions.description: Meaningful support for tension headaches, migraines, and persistent head pain—approached without reliance on medication.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM recognizes several headache patterns, including Liver Yang rising, Blood deficiency, and external pathogenic influences. We assess your individual pattern and tailor treatment using acupuncture and herbal medicine accordingly.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorders
conditions.description: Supportive, drug-free care for anxiety, panic attacks, and persistent feelings of nervousness.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, anxiety is often connected to Heart and Kidney disharmony, Liver Qi stagnation, or disturbance of the Shen (spirit). Our treatments work to calm the Shen, restore balanced Qi flow, and nourish the relevant organs to support emotional stability.

conditions.title: Depression & Low Mood
symptoms: A sense of hopelessness or feeling without purpose
conditions.description: Whole-person support for those experiencing depression, persistent low energy, and reduced motivation.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, depression is often linked to Liver Qi stagnation, Heart Blood deficiency, or Spleen Qi deficiency. Care is directed toward releasing stagnant Qi, addressing underlying deficiencies, and supporting emotional wellbeing.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Disorders
conditions.description: Supportive, natural approaches for those struggling to fall asleep, stay asleep, or achieve restorative sleep quality.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, sleep difficulties frequently arise from Heart and Kidney imbalance, Yin deficiency with internal heat, or disturbance of the Shen. Our care focuses on nourishing Yin, settling the Heart, and anchoring the Shen to support deeper, more restful sleep.

conditions.title: Stress & Burnout
conditions.description: When chronic stress or burnout has taken a toll, we work with you to rebuild balance and strengthen your resilience.
conditions.tcmApproach: Prolonged stress tends to exhaust Qi and promote Liver stagnation. Our approach addresses this by regulating the Liver, tonifying areas of deficiency, and incorporating stress management strategies that help restore equilibrium and build lasting resilience.

conditions.title: IBS & Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Supportive care for irritable bowel syndrome, bloating, gas, and general digestive discomfort.
conditions.tcmApproach: IBS commonly reflects a Liver-Spleen disharmony in which emotional stress disrupts digestive function. Treatment works to regulate Liver Qi, reinforce Spleen function, and bring the digestive system back into harmony.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux & GERD
conditions.description: Natural care options for heartburn, acid reflux, and gastroesophageal reflux disease.
conditions.tcmApproach: Reflux is understood in TCM as Stomach Qi rebelling in an upward direction, frequently accompanied by heat or phlegm. Treatment aims to redirect Stomach Qi downward, clear excess heat, and support overall digestive strength.

conditions.title: Constipation
symptoms: Less frequent bowel movements than usual
conditions.description: Gentle and thoughtful support for those dealing with chronic constipation or irregular bowel habits.
conditions.tcmApproach: Constipation can stem from several TCM patterns, including Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, Yin deficiency, or Spleen Qi weakness. Your care plan is tailored to your individual pattern, with the goal of restoring regular bowel function.

conditions.title: Fertility Support
conditions.description: Whole-person support for natural conception and IVF preparation, tailored to both women and men.
conditions.tcmApproach: Our approach centers on balancing menstrual cycles, nourishing Kidney Essence (Jing), enhancing blood flow to the reproductive organs, and easing stress. Each partner receives a care plan designed around their unique needs.

conditions.title: Menstrual Disorders
conditions.description: Natural care for painful periods, irregular cycles, PMS, and other menstrual-related concerns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Menstrual concerns frequently point to Liver Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, or Cold in the uterus. Treatment works to regulate the cycle, nourish Blood, move stagnant Qi, and address underlying pathogenic factors.

conditions.title: Menopause Symptoms
conditions.description: Supportive, natural care for hot flashes, night sweats, mood shifts, and other symptoms associated with menopause.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM views menopause as a gradual decline in Kidney Yin and Yang. By nourishing Kidney Yin, bringing Yin and Yang into greater harmony, and calming the Shen, we aim to ease this life transition and alleviate associated symptoms.

conditions.title: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)
conditions.description: A whole-person approach to supporting hormone balance, restoring regular cycles, and managing PCOS symptoms.
conditions.tcmApproach: PCOS is understood in TCM as a combination of Kidney deficiency, Phlegm-Damp accumulation, and Liver Qi stagnation. Care is focused on resolving phlegm, moving and regulating Qi, and strengthening Kidney function.

conditions.title: Allergies & Hay Fever
conditions.description: Natural support for seasonal allergies, hay fever, and sensitivities to environmental triggers.
conditions.tcmApproach: Allergic responses are often rooted in weakness of Wei Qi (defensive Qi) alongside internal imbalances. Treatment works to fortify Wei Qi, clear pathogenic influences, and help reduce inflammatory reactions.

conditions.title: Asthma
conditions.description: Complementary care to support asthma management, with a focus on reducing how often and how severely episodes occur.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, asthma is often rooted in deficiency of the Lung and Kidney, combined with phlegm accumulation. Our approach focuses on supporting Lung and Kidney function, clearing phlegm, and calming respiratory reactivity.

conditions.title: Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Care for a persistent cough that has not improved with conventional treatments.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, chronic cough can arise from several distinct patterns — among them Lung Yin deficiency, Lung Heat, or the buildup of phlegm. By carefully identifying which pattern applies to you, we tailor treatment to address your specific presentation.
treatmentMethods: Remedies to soothe and support the throat

conditions.title: Frequent Colds & Flu
conditions.description: Support your immune system to help lower how often you get sick and ease the severity of colds and respiratory infections.
conditions.tcmApproach: Getting sick often is a sign of Wei Qi deficiency in TCM. We work to strengthen Wei Qi, support Lung and Spleen function, and build lasting immune resilience throughout the body.

conditions.title: Chronic Fatigue
symptoms: Struggling to get through everyday tasks
conditions.description: When persistent tiredness and low energy are affecting your daily life, we focus on helping you rebuild vitality and restore a sense of wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: Chronic fatigue commonly points to Qi deficiency in the Spleen, Kidney, or both — and may also involve Blood deficiency. We address these underlying deficiencies directly, working to restore your energy and overall vitality.
treatmentMethods: Guidance on balancing rest with physical activity

introduction.note: Not finding your condition here? Reach out to us — there's a good chance we can still support you.
introduction.text: At Dr. Maya Chen Clinic, our focus is on the whole person — not simply the symptoms. By looking at the root causes of illness alongside your immediate concerns, we take a thorough, integrative approach to your care. A selection of the conditions we commonly work with is outlined below.
